code_fixing_patterns.overview: Practical patterns and examples for fixing code after package upgrades. This guide provides concrete solutions for common breaking changes and best practices for implementing fixes.

tool_usage_guidelines.title: Using Bob's Tools Effectively for Code Fixes

tool.name: apply_diff
tool.when_to_use: - Making surgical edits to existing code - Changing specific lines or functions - Multiple related changes in one file - When you know exact content to replace

best_practices.practice: Always read the file first to get exact content
Make multiple changes in one diff when possible
Use multiple SEARCH/REPLACE blocks for different sections
Ensure SEARCH content matches exactly (including whitespace)

example.scenario: Fixing import statement and function call
example.code_template: apply_diff with path and multiple SEARCH/REPLACE blocks for import changes and API updates

tool.name: insert_content
tool.when_to_use: - Adding new imports - Adding new functions or classes - Inserting configuration blocks

example.scenario: Adding new import at top of file
example.code_template: insert_content at line 1 for new imports

scenario.approach: 1. Use search_files to find all import statements 2. Read each file with the old import 3. Use apply_diff to update the import path 4. Add comment explaining the change
1. Update import statement 2. Find all usages of OldClass 3. Replace with NewClass throughout file 4. Update any type hints or docstrings

scenario.steps: 1. Identify all calls to the function 2. Determine appropriate value for new parameter 3. Update each call site 4. Consider if default value is appropriate
1. Search for all keyword argument usages 2. Update parameter name 3. Verify functionality unchanged
1. Remove the parameter from all calls 2. Check if removed parameter had side effects 3. Implement alternative if needed

pattern.name: deprecated_api_replacement
pattern.description: Old API deprecated, new API available

scenario.steps: 1. Find all usages of old_method 2. Check if new_method has same signature 3. Update all call sites 4. Verify return values are compatible 5. Add comment referencing deprecation
1. Identify the old pattern 2. Understand the new pattern 3. Refactor code to use new pattern 4. Test that behavior is preserved

pattern.name: return_type_change
pattern.description: Function return type or structure changed

scenario.steps: 1. Identify all places using the return value 2. Change dictionary access to attribute access 3. Update any type hints 4. Handle potential AttributeErrors
1. Determine if list is actually needed 2. If yes, wrap in list() 3. If no, use generator directly 4. Update any code expecting list methods
1. Identify all configuration dictionaries 2. Add new required options 3. Use appropriate default values 4. Document why values were chosen
1. Understand new structure 2. Migrate existing config 3. Update all config access points 4. Test configuration loading
1. Identify new exception types 2. Update exception handlers 3. Consider if different handling needed 4. Update error messages if needed

pattern.name: type_hints_update
pattern.description: Update type hints for new package versions

pattern.name: async_await_changes
pattern.description: Function changed from sync to async
pattern.note: May require making calling function async as well

step.details: - Read error message carefully - Understand what's expected vs what's happening - Locate the exact line causing the issue
- Check package documentation - Review migration guide - Look at package examples - Verify the new API

step.action: Read the file to be modified
step.details: Get exact content including whitespace and indentation

step.considerations: - What lines need to change? - Are there multiple occurrences? - Will this affect other code? - Do imports need updating?

step.details: - Use exact content from read_file - Include line numbers - Make all related changes together - Add explanatory comments
Run the specific test to confirm it passes
Run full test suite to ensure no new failures

practice.name: minimal_changes
practice.rationale: - Easier to review - Less risk of introducing bugs - Clearer what was changed and why
practice.example: Good: Update only the specific function call that changed Bad: Refactor entire file while fixing one issue

practice.name: preserve_functionality
practice.rationale: The goal is to fix tests, not change functionality
practice.verification: - Tests should pass with same assertions - Output should be identical - Side effects should be preserved

practice.name: add_comments
practice.format: # Updated for package v2.0 - function signature changed # See: https://package.docs/migration-guide
practice.benefits: - Future maintainers understand the change - Links to documentation for reference - Explains non-obvious decisions

practice.name: consistent_style
practice.considerations: - Indentation (spaces vs tabs) - Quote style (single vs double) - Line length - Naming conventions

practice.name: test_incrementally
practice.workflow: 1. Make one fix 2. Run affected tests 3. Verify they pass 4. Move to next fix
practice.benefit: Catch issues immediately rather than debugging multiple changes

issue.name: fix_doesnt_work
issue.symptom: Applied fix but test still fails
issue.diagnosis: - Verify the fix was applied correctly - Check if there are multiple occurrences - Ensure you're testing the right thing - Verify package version is correct

solutions.solution: Re-read the file to confirm changes
Search for other occurrences of the issue

issue.name: new_failures_appear
issue.symptom: Fix causes other tests to fail
issue.diagnosis: - The fix had unintended side effects - Changed behavior in unexpected ways - Broke assumptions in other code

solutions.solution: Review what else uses the changed code
May need to update related code
Check if fix is too broad

issue.name: cant_find_replacement
issue.symptom: Don't know what to replace deprecated API with
issue.diagnosis: - Documentation unclear - No obvious replacement - Multiple possible solutions

solutions.solution: Look at package test files for examples
Search GitHub issues for migration help
Check if there's a compatibility layer

fix_documentation_template.title: Documenting Your Fixes
